_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Abnormal pre-malignant/malignant growth on the eyelid
Abnormal lesion/lump on the eyelid confirmed as benign/malignant on excision biopsy
Associated with chronic ultraviolet light/sun exposure (Basal cell, squamous cell, sebaceous gland, melanoma)

preauth.label: Clinical Photograph of the affected eye

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed clinical notes, including history, clinical signs and symptoms, physical examination/investigations and indication, available?
Clinical photo of affected part with proper labelling (R/L) and full face photograph available?

cpd_claim.q: Documents available to show appropriate post-op care and follow-up advice?
OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of surgery?
Documentary evidence present for histopathology examination of specimen?

common_queries: Histopathology report missing for Lid Tumor excision + Lid Reconstruction
Affected eye not labelled R or L in the clinical photograph for Lid Tumor excision + Lid Reconstruction
Diagnosis not backed by clinical signs/symptoms/ophthalmic examination or conservative therapy not attempted/failed for Lid Tumor excision
Surgical specimen not sent for histopathology or report missing/inconclusive, especially if lesion is atypical

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Lid Surgeries only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
In Lid tumor excision the surgical specimen should be sent for histopathology, if lesion is atypical.
